title: veronica serpyllifolia subsp. parvifolia
intro: Veronica serpyllifolia subsp. parvifolia, commonly known as small-leaved speedwell or parvifolia speedwell, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. This plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting skin health due to its high antioxidant content. In herbal preparations, Veronica serpyllifolia subsp. parvifolia can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
